verses.text: I fear lest, as 'the serpent beguiled Eve,' your minds be corrupted 'from the simplicity that is in Christ.' Guard the heart. Simple devotion to Christ is easily led astray.
I fear lest, as 'the serpent beguiled Eve,' your minds be corrupted 'from the simplicity that is in Christ.' Guard the heart. Simple devotion to Christ is easily led astray.
I fear lest, as 'the serpent beguiled Eve,' your minds be corrupted 'from the simplicity that is in Christ.' Guard the heart. Simple devotion to Christ is easily led astray.
Ye bear with another gospel; 'I was not a whit behind the very chiefest apostles.' Defended. He upholds his apostleship against false teachers.
Ye bear with another gospel; 'I was not a whit behind the very chiefest apostles.' Defended. He upholds his apostleship against false teachers.
Ye bear with another gospel; 'I was not a whit behind the very chiefest apostles.' Defended. He upholds his apostleship against false teachers.
I preached freely, taking from other churches, 'chargeable to no man' among you. Free of charge. He took no support, to commend the gospel.
I preached freely, taking from other churches, 'chargeable to no man' among you. Free of charge. He took no support, to commend the gospel.
I preached freely, taking from other churches, 'chargeable to no man' among you. Free of charge. He took no support, to commend the gospel.
I will keep this boasting, to cut off occasion from false apostles. No occasion given. He guards his motives from slander.
I will keep this boasting, to cut off occasion from false apostles. No occasion given. He guards his motives from slander.
I will keep this boasting, to cut off occasion from false apostles. No occasion given. He guards his motives from slander.
False apostles transform themselves; 'Satan himself is transformed into an angel of light.' Deceivers. The enemy's servants wear a fair disguise.
False apostles transform themselves; 'Satan himself is transformed into an angel of light.' Deceivers. The enemy's servants wear a fair disguise.
False apostles transform themselves; 'Satan himself is transformed into an angel of light.' Deceivers. The enemy's servants wear a fair disguise.
Let none think me a fool; since many glory after the flesh, I will glory too. Forced boasting. Reluctantly he must answer their boasts.
Let none think me a fool; since many glory after the flesh, I will glory too. Forced boasting. Reluctantly he must answer their boasts.
Let none think me a fool; since many glory after the flesh, I will glory too. Forced boasting. Reluctantly he must answer their boasts.
Ye suffer fools gladly; whereinsoever any is bold, I am bold also. Bearing fools. He matches their boasting to expose it.
Ye suffer fools gladly; whereinsoever any is bold, I am bold also. Bearing fools. He matches their boasting to expose it.
Ye suffer fools gladly; whereinsoever any is bold, I am bold also. Bearing fools. He matches their boasting to expose it.
Are they Hebrews? so am I; ministers of Christ? 'I am more,' in labours and stripes. Greater toil. His sufferings prove his service.
Are they Hebrews? so am I; ministers of Christ? 'I am more,' in labours and stripes. Greater toil. His sufferings prove his service.
Five times forty stripes save one; thrice beaten, once stoned, thrice shipwrecked. Many sufferings. He bore wound on wound for Christ.
Five times forty stripes save one; thrice beaten, once stoned, thrice shipwrecked. Many sufferings. He bore wound on wound for Christ.
In journeys, perils of waters, robbers, kinsmen; in weariness, hunger, and cold. Constant peril. Danger followed him everywhere.
In journeys, perils of waters, robbers, kinsmen; in weariness, hunger, and cold. Constant peril. Danger followed him everywhere.
Beside these, 'the care of all the churches' presses daily; who is weak, and I am not weak? Burdened heart. He felt every believer's trouble.
Beside these, 'the care of all the churches' presses daily; who is weak, and I am not weak? Burdened heart. He felt every believer's trouble.
If I must glory, I glory of my infirmities; at Damascus I was let down the wall in a basket. Humbling escape. He boasts of weakness, not triumph.
If I must glory, I glory of my infirmities; at Damascus I was let down the wall in a basket. Humbling escape. He boasts of weakness, not triumph.
If I must glory, I glory of my infirmities; at Damascus I was let down the wall in a basket. Humbling escape. He boasts of weakness, not triumph.
